meta.setting: County rooms, courthouse steps, mourning houses, schoolrooms, and back kitchens around Shasta and French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with testimony, land papers, public shame, law heat, family pressure, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Courthouse, restored 1861 context, county records, mining commerce, iron shutters, and old town material.

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Regional settlement, mining communities, families, roads, and social pressure.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Displacement and allotment pressure, reminding the generated page to treat official records as contested.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Courthouse Memory Women cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented docket note, mourning-house warning, or witness saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Record Keepers and Widows
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing women who keep court memory, deeds, testimony, mourning, and unofficial truth alive
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or habits
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate this page around clerks, widows, interpreters, washerwomen, teachers, cooks, daughters, and witnesses whose memory challenges the official record.
Tie future moves to testimony, document recovery, social leverage, funeral knowledge, land disputes, public shame, hidden kindness, and remembering what a judge ignored.
Avoid sentimental sainthood; give them anger, humor, fatigue, practical limits, and sharp recall.
Make official papers useful but suspect, especially around land, labor, women, Native families, and company money.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: They should feel like people who can fold laundry, quote a docket, and ruin a liar before supper. The courthouse writes the record; these women remember what the courthouse left out.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A courthouse-memory scene should ask who signed, who witnessed, who was not allowed to speak, and who still remembers the room.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western courthouse and domestic labor language only.
Keep memory practical, social, and politically useful.
The generated page must include image path cast/images/courthouse-memory-women.jpg.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred, or fake-tribal framing
do not flatten women into saints, victims, or gossip devices
do not use modern slang, modern office gear, vehicles, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about testimony, records, mourning, unofficial truth, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
